content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 27, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on June 27 focused on cloud infrastructure hardening, security audits, and improving the computer-use plugin. Key achievements include implementing a flag-gated shared-tier cloud agent, resolving macOS window listing bugs, and advancing the multi-tenant security audit for cloud applications.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Cloud Infrastructure & App Provisioning**
*   Implemented a flag-gated tier flip for the cloud app, allowing users to provision shared (in-Worker) cloud agents on first-run [#9838](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9838).
*   Cleaned up cloud UI components by removing dead image-generation code and redundant in-dashboard quick-chat features [#9835](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9835), [#9834](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9834).
*   Aligned cloud URL canonicalization and updated plugin documentation to match apex Eliza Cloud standards [#9832](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9832).

**Security & Core Fixes**
*   Completed Steward OAuth login by correctly handling URL fragments to prevent leakage in server logs [#9831](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9831).
*   Updated `CharacterInput` to correctly support `TemplateType` (functions) for template definitions [#9839](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9839).

**Computer Use & Ecosystem Expansion**
*   Repaired macOS window listing and achieved full input coverage for on-device Computer Use Agent (CUA) verification [#9820](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9820).
*   Added a third-party registry entry for `plugin-x402-endpoints`, providing access to 28 paid global data endpoints [#9837](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9837).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   No new pull requests were opened on this date.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   No issues were closed on this date.

## NEW ISSUES
**Cloud Platform Hardening**
*   [#9853](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9853): Initiated a work order for multi-tenant hardening and audit completion for cloud apps, focusing on container deployment and per-tenant database isolation.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

**[#9580](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9580) On-device inference: non-Android backend verification matrix**
*   **Progress:** Extensive adversarial verification has been completed for the P1 security core. The SSRF `safeFetch` implementation and isolated-DB fail-closed mechanisms are confirmed sound.
*   **Challenges:** Developers identified latent risks in per-IP rate limiting (TOCTOU race conditions) and structural blind spots in tenant-scope checks for `and`/`or` query combinators.
*   **Next Steps:** Focus is shifting to P2 items, including daemon health monitoring and end-to-end verification of money paths (payouts and registrar), with specific fixes flagged for rate-limiting and tenant-isolation gates.
